  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "Systems incorporating language model components must decide how much to spend on verification and where to spend it. This paper provides the cost surface for that decision.",

  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "We have shown that epistemic honesty is unverifiable for systems operating under text-only observation models with bounded supervision. It is a structural property of the interface: the channel through which these systems communicate discards the epistemic metadata that would make verification possible.",
    "suggested_text": "Epistemic honesty is unverifiable for systems operating under text-only observation models with bounded supervision. This is a structural property of the interface: their communication channel discards the epistemic metadata needed for verification.",
    "words_saved": 17,
    "explanation": "Removes conversational 'We have shown that' and tightens the second sentence by replacing wordy clauses ('the channel through which...', 'that would make... possible') with concise equivalents."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "This impossibility establishes the floor of the cost surface: below this line, text-channel investment cannot improve assurance.",
    "suggested_text": "This impossibility establishes the cost surface floor: investment in the text channel cannot improve assurance.",
    "words_saved": 8,
    "explanation": "Shortens 'the floor of the cost surface' and removes the redundant clause 'below this line', which is already implied by 'floor'."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "Our epistemic observability tensor interface demonstrates this escape is achievable with current architectures and minimal overhead.",
    "suggested_text": "Our epistemic observability tensor interface achieves this escape with current architectures and minimal overhead.",
    "words_saved": 3,
    "explanation": "Replaces a wordy, passive-leaning verb phrase ('demonstrates...is achievable') with a stronger, more active verb ('achieves')."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "Under bounded verification, tensor-guided judges at 10% budget outperform text-guided judges at 30% budget, a 3x reduction in verification budget for equivalent accuracy.",
    "suggested_text": "Under bounded verification, tensor-guided judges achieve equivalent accuracy to text-guided judges with a 3x smaller budget (10% vs. 30%).",
    "words_saved": 8,
    "explanation": "Rephrases to state the main finding (equivalent accuracy, smaller budget) more directly, using the numbers as parenthetical support rather than repeating the 3x calculation."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "The engineering question is not whether to verify, but how much verification to buy and at which tier.",
    "suggested_text": "The engineering question is not whether to verify, but how much to buy and at which tier.",
